title: Literature-style prose
description: Classic literary paragraph — rhythm, imagery, depth
text: It was a clear, cold afternoon in late October, the kind of day on which the trees seem to glow from inside. She walked along the river path, her hands tucked into her coat pockets, and listened to the leaves crunching softly under her boots. The world, for once, felt entirely her own.

tips.text: Good prose reads slow and types fast. Let the sentence set the pace.
Imagery and long adjectives are where accuracy slips. Hold it steady.

tips.target: It was a clear, cold afternoon in late October, the kind of day on which the trees seem to glow from inside. She walked along the

tips.text: Read the line in your head first. Then let your hands follow it.

tips.target: river path, her hands tucked into her coat pockets, and listened to the leaves crunching softly under her boots. The world, for once, felt entirely her own.

character_tips.anna: Prose at this speed is a fine thing. Most writers never type their own copy this cleanly.
character_tips.maxim: This is the working speed of an editor on deadline. You're keeping it.
character_tips.knopych: A good paragraph, carried at pace. Mind the rhythm and it carries itself.
character_tips.klavochka: Type it the way you'd read it aloud to someone you respect: clear, unhurried, exact.
